meta.structure_note: All color custom properties in this codebase live in exactly one file (frontend/app/globals.css), declared twice each: once in :root (light theme) and once in [data-theme="dark"] (dark theme overrides). There are two parallel, coexisting palettes rather than one: a 'core' set (--color-*, the original app-wide primitives used in chat/login/pricing/shared badges) and an 'admin' set (--admin-*, a second palette ported from a separate 'Theke Admin design' prototype and layered on top of the core set for the sidebar, top bar, and every admin-facing panel/dashboard). This file preserves that real split (tokens.core / tokens.admin / tokens.chat) instead of forcing everything into a single flat list.
changelog_since_last_export: Removed 3 dead tokens that were declared but never referenced: --color-text-on-accent, --admin-construction-on-dark, --admin-tax-on-dark. (A 4th dead token, --gradient-sidebar, was also removed from the CSS but was never part of this JSON export - it's a gradient, not a flat color.)

Unified --admin-danger and --error-red (both were #c62828 in light mode, and both fell back to the same value in dark mode) into a single --admin-danger token. Survivor chosen for broader existing usage (admin badges, delete/suspend confirmations, failed-sync status - 13 call sites vs. --error-red's 6 form-validation-only call sites) and because it doesn't bake a hue into the token's own name.

Unified --admin-info and --color-primary-hover (both were #2a6fdb in light mode, and this pairing was actually already an intentional documented choice, not an accidental duplicate) into a single --color-primary-hover token. Survivor chosen because it's the foundational core primitive powering the universal .btn-primary:hover rule and already had a proper, tested dark-mode value (#5c7dc4) - renaming that foundational rule to an --admin-* token would have been an architectural regression even though --admin-info had more raw call sites.

Added genuine dark-mode values for admin.status.success (#2e7d32 -> #4ade80) and admin.status.warning (#f57f17 -> #fb923c), matching the brightening pattern their core.status siblings already use. admin.status.danger is explicitly restated with the SAME value in both themes (deliberate - matches core.status.danger's own established 'constant across themes' pattern, not an oversight).

Declared --color-surface-hover properly (was previously referenced with an undeclared fallback, rgba(0,0,0,0.04), invisible in dark mode). New value: rgba(21,29,72,0.05) light / rgba(255,255,255,0.07) dark.

Added a new token, admin.text.text_on_dark (--admin-text-on-dark), consolidating three previously ad hoc hardcoded values (#fff, #b7c2dc, #d8e0f0) used for secondary/body text on permanently-dark navy surfaces (sidebar footer role label + sign-out icon, DataSourcesPanel's active frequency-pill label, VerticalEditorPanel's system-prompt textarea text). Genuinely maximal-contrast name/heading text on the same surfaces (the user's own name in the sidebar footer, the avatar initial) was left pointed at the existing core.text.on_primary token instead, preserving the footer's existing two-tier visual hierarchy rather than flattening it.

Fixed a hardcoded #d8d0c2 (a leftover from the old warm-beige palette) on the collapsed sidebar's inactive vertical-switcher dot - now uses admin.surface.card_border, the sidebar's own established border token.
Net token count: 59 -> 56 (-3 dead, -1 danger merge, -1 info merge, +1 surface_hover, +1 text_on_dark).

Follow-up pass: swept the whole frontend for remaining raw hex in CSS/inline-style contexts (every one was a 'text/icon sitting on a colored fill' case, same role as core.text.on_primary) and repointed them at that existing token instead of adding anything new: Sidebar.tsx's vertical-switcher active-segment text+dot, Sidebar.module.css's .navChildItemActive text and .avatar border, TopHeader.module.css/StatCard.module.css/AttentionCard.module.css's avatar/icon-badge text, SessionExpiryToast.module.css and SubscriptionsPanel.module.css's status-pill text, and two inline danger-button labels in CompaniesPanel.tsx/DocumentsPanel.tsx. Also replaced a hand-picked dark-theme background (Sidebar.module.css's .navItemActive, was a hardcoded #25344f) with the same color-mix(...) pattern the light-theme rule right above it already uses, just mixed against the dark surface instead of white - ties it back to --admin-accent-navy instead of an unrelated hex. Removed two now-redundant var(..., #fallback) hex fallbacks in pricing.module.css (the token they fell back from is always declared).

Correction to the follow-up pass above: 2 of the call sites it repointed at core.text.on_primary were actually background/border properties, not text - Sidebar.tsx's .switcherDotInline active fill and Sidebar.module.css's .avatar ring border. A background/border color and a text color are different design-system concerns even when the current hex happens to match (#ffffff in both cases here), so a rebrand could need to change one without the other. Split those 2 call sites out into a new token, core.background.on_primary, rather than continuing to borrow the text token. Grouped both into ONE new token (not two) since they share identical value and semantic role; core.text.on_primary's remaining ~11 call sites were re-verified as genuine `color:`/icon-foreground usages and were left as-is.
Net token count: 56 -> 57 (+1 core.background.on_primary; no removals).

excluded_non_variable_colors.note: The Logo/LogoMark brand-mark green. Hardcoded on purpose per an explicit code comment ('Colors are fixed per the brand spec regardless of light/dark theme') - it is not a CSS variable and editing this JSON will not change it.

excluded_non_variable_colors.found_in: frontend/app/components/MapPicker.tsx (PIN_NAVY, PIN_AMBER constants, plus the white glyph fills/strokes inside the pin SVG markup itself)
excluded_non_variable_colors.note: Map pin marker colors, duplicated as plain JS string literals (some inside a template-literal SVG string) because Leaflet's divIcon needs literal markup, not a CSS var(). Still un-tokenized after this cleanup pass - out of scope (Leaflet API constraint, not a CSS bug).
Browser-chrome theme-color meta tag, duplicated as a literal because Next.js metadata is evaluated at build time. Only has a light-mode value.

meta.instructions: Edit only the 'value' fields below. Do not rename keys, remove entries, or change structure. Return this same file with updated hex values.

light.usage: Page background behind every card/surface

dark.usage: Page background behind every card/surface

light.usage: Default card/panel/input background

dark.usage: Default card/panel/input background

light.usage: Secondary/inset surface - table stripes, chips, citation pills, empty-state icon tiles

dark.usage: Secondary/inset surface - table stripes, chips, citation pills, empty-state icon tiles

light.usage: Subtle hover-state tint over core.background.surface (e.g. CustomerCombobox result rows). A translucent overlay, not a flat hex, so it composites correctly over whatever surface it sits on.

dark.usage: Subtle hover-state tint over core.background.surface (light-on-dark direction, since a dark overlay barely registers on an already-dark card)

surface_hover.note: NEWLY DECLARED - previously referenced as var(--color-surface-hover, rgba(0,0,0,0.04)) with no real declaration behind it, so every theme silently used the same near-invisible-in-dark-mode fallback.

light.usage: Small decorative fill/border sitting on top of a solid colored surface - a switcher dot's active fill, an avatar's ring border. Deliberately a SEPARATE token from core.text.on_primary even though both are currently #ffffff: a background/border color and a text color are different concerns, not the same value wearing two hats - a previous cleanup pass had incorrectly pointed these two call sites at the text token just because the hex matched.

dark.usage: Same decorative fill/border color, not overridden in dark theme

on_primary.note: NEW - split out from core.text.on_primary, see usage note.

light.usage: Default 1px hairline border on cards, inputs, dividers

dark.usage: Default 1px hairline border on cards, inputs, dividers

light.usage: Default body/heading text color

dark.usage: Default body/heading text color

light.usage: Secondary/caption text, placeholders, .text-muted utility class

dark.usage: Secondary/caption text, placeholders, .text-muted utility class

light.usage: Text/icon color (the color CSS property specifically - not background or border) for content sitting on any solid colored fill: primary buttons, user chat bubble, sidebar avatar initial + footer name, status-pill text, admin danger/warning confirm-button labels. Broadened from just core.brand.primary during a follow-up sweep - but kept strictly to actual `color:`/icon-foreground usages; the two call sites that were actually background/border properties (a switcher dot's fill, the avatar's ring border) were moved to the new core.background.on_primary instead, since a background color and a text color are different concerns even when the value matches.

dark.usage: Text/icon color for content sitting on any solid colored fill
used_in: same call sites as light - not overridden in dark theme

light.usage: Primary navy - fill for primary buttons, active states, user chat bubble, avatar background; matches admin.brand.accent_navy exactly

dark.usage: Primary navy - fill for primary buttons, active states, user chat bubble, avatar background

light.usage: Hover state for primary-filled buttons/backgrounds. Also the color used for admin 'info/syncing' status UI (DataSourcesPanel's syncing indicator + source-tier badges, sources.module.css) - unified from a separate --admin-info token that held the identical value.

dark.usage: Hover state for primary-filled buttons/backgrounds; also the admin info/syncing status color

light.usage: Foreground use for links/active tabs/focus rings - aliases core.brand.primary in light mode (huge contrast against white already)

dark.usage: Foreground use for links/active tabs/focus rings - a lighter periwinkle than core.brand.primary specifically because the dark-mode primary (#3e5a9e) fails WCAG AA (~2.5:1) as text/border color

light.usage: Hover state for link-colored text/foreground elements

dark.usage: Hover state for link-colored text/foreground elements

light.usage: Amber accent - identical to core.status.warning in light mode

dark.usage: Amber accent - identical to core.status.warning in dark mode

light.usage: Hover state for core.brand.accent fills

dark.usage: Hover state for core.brand.accent fills

light.usage: Success text/icon color (healthy status, positive deltas)

dark.usage: Success text/icon color (healthy status, positive deltas)

light.usage: Background tint behind success text/badges (.badge-success)

dark.usage: Background tint behind success text/badges (.badge-success)

light.usage: Warning text/icon color (needs-attention status)

dark.usage: Warning text/icon color (needs-attention status)

light.usage: Background tint behind warning text/badges, disclaimers

dark.usage: Background tint behind warning text/badges, disclaimers

light.usage: Danger/error text/icon color (critical status, destructive actions). Lighter coral-red than admin.status.danger by design - two deliberately distinct reds for banners/badges (this one) vs. admin panels + form validation (that one).

dark.usage: Danger/error text/icon color - not overridden in dark theme

light.usage: Background tint behind danger text/badges

dark.usage: Background tint behind danger text/badges

light.usage: Teal - neutral/informational stat tone, second ActivityChart line color, distinct hue from core.brand.primary's blue family

dark.usage: Teal - neutral/informational stat tone

light.usage: Background tint behind info text/badges

dark.usage: Background tint behind info text/badges

light.usage: Purple accent tone for a 4th badge/stat-card family (documents/knowledge-base icons)

dark.usage: Purple accent tone for a 4th badge/stat-card family

light.usage: Background tint behind purple text/badges

dark.usage: Background tint behind purple text/badges

light.usage: Top header bar background across admin/app-shell screens

dark.usage: Top header bar background across admin/app-shell screens

light.usage: Main app-shell content-area background (AppShell), the post-login page canvas behind the sidebar/topbar

dark.usage: Main app-shell content-area background

light.usage: Fixed-dark background for the vertical-editor's raw system-prompt textarea - stays dark in both themes on purpose so the 'advanced/raw' surface reads consistently

dark.usage: Same fixed-dark background, not overridden in dark theme (by design)

light.usage: Border for admin-shell cards/sidebar edge (cooler-toned sibling of core.border.default). Also used for the collapsed sidebar's inactive vertical-switcher dot border (previously a hardcoded leftover #d8d0c2 from the old warm-beige palette).

dark.usage: Border for admin-shell cards/sidebar edge

light.usage: Divider between list rows / nav sections in admin screens

dark.usage: Divider between list rows / nav sections in admin screens

light.usage: Pill/chip background (nav hover, TypeBadge neutral chips, font-scale control)

dark.usage: Pill/chip background

light.usage: Admin-screen heading/icon text tone - inverts to near-white in dark mode (unlike admin.brand.accent_navy, which brightens instead)

dark.usage: Admin-screen heading/icon text tone - near-white in dark mode

light.usage: Muted label/caption text in admin screens (cool-gray sibling of core.text.muted)

dark.usage: Muted label/caption text in admin screens

light.usage: Default/inactive body text - nav item labels, vertical-switcher segments (a third text tone distinct from heading_navy and muted_stone)

dark.usage: Default/inactive body text

light.usage: Readable secondary/body text for content sitting on a permanently-dark navy surface: sidebar footer role label + sign-out icon, DataSourcesPanel's active frequency-pill label, VerticalEditorPanel's system-prompt textarea text. Same value in both app themes - exists specifically for surfaces that stay dark regardless of the app's own light/dark theme.

dark.usage: Same as light - deliberately constant across themes

text_on_dark.note: NEWLY ADDED - previously three separate ad hoc hardcoded values (#fff, #b7c2dc, #d8e0f0) with nothing connecting them. The footer's own NAME/avatar-initial text was deliberately left on core.text.on_primary instead of this token, preserving the existing prominent-name / muted-role two-tier hierarchy rather than flattening both to the same tone.

light.usage: Filled/active surface navy - sidebar footer, active nav-child pill, 'All' vertical-switcher segment, sync-now buttons. Theme-adaptive fill (brightens rather than inverting, unlike heading_navy)

dark.usage: Filled/active surface navy

light.usage: Construction-vertical accent (badge text/icon, dashboard card left-border and pill)

dark.usage: Construction-vertical accent

light.usage: Background tint behind the construction badge/pill

dark.usage: Background tint behind the construction badge/pill

light.usage: Tax & Accounting-vertical accent ('Tax bronze' - a deliberate, previously-confirmed convention distinct from the design-review mock's proposed amber)

dark.usage: Tax & Accounting-vertical accent

light.usage: Background tint behind the tax badge/pill

dark.usage: Background tint behind the tax badge/pill

light.usage: Neutral/default badge tone in admin panels - identical value to admin.brand.accent_navy

dark.usage: Neutral/default badge tone in admin panels

light.usage: Background tint behind the neutral admin badge

dark.usage: Background tint behind the neutral admin badge

light.usage: Admin-panel success tone (e.g. healthy data-source sync status)

dark.usage: Admin-panel success tone - NEWLY brightened for dark mode (was previously inheriting the light value unchanged), matching the direction core.status.success already brightens.

light.usage: Admin-panel warning tone (e.g. overdue data-source sync)

dark.usage: Admin-panel warning tone - NEWLY brightened for dark mode, staying in the orange family (distinct from core.status.warning's own, more yellow, dark tone) while matching the sibling brightening direction.

light.usage: Background tint behind the admin warning tone/badge

dark.usage: Background tint behind the admin warning tone/badge

light.usage: Border for admin warning callouts/disclaimers

dark.usage: Border for admin warning callouts/disclaimers

light.usage: Admin-panel danger tone (delete/suspend confirmations, failed-sync status) AND inline field-validation borders/text app-wide. Unified from two previously separate tokens (--admin-danger, --error-red) that always held the identical value.

dark.usage: Same value in both themes - deliberate, matching core.status.danger's own established 'constant across themes' pattern, restated explicitly rather than left as an implicit cascade fallback.

light.usage: Background of the user's own chat message bubble - reuses core.brand.primary, not a dedicated token

dark.usage: Background of the user's own chat message bubble

light.usage: Text color inside the user's chat bubble - reuses core.text.on_primary

dark.usage: Text color inside the user's chat bubble

dark.usage: Background of each source-citation pill

dark.usage: Background of the disclaimer strip

dark.usage: Bottom border of the disclaimer strip
